edges.via_infra: CDN / Edge Cache → Web Application Firewall
edges.story_text: As a end user, I want to communicate between Citizens / Browser and eServices Web Portal (via CDN / Edge Cache → Web Application Firewall) to support the workflow. If this service path is compromised, an attacker can pivot laterally to eServices Web Portal (via CDN / Edge Cache → Web Application Firewall).
edges.exploitation_consequence: this service path is compromised, an attacker can pivot laterally to eServices Web Portal (via CDN / Edge Cache → Web Application Firewall).

edges.via_infra: CDN / Edge Cache → Web Application Firewall
edges.story_text: As a end user, I want to communicate between Mobile App iOS/Android and eServices Web Portal (via CDN / Edge Cache → Web Application Firewall) to support the workflow. If this service path is compromised, an attacker can pivot laterally to eServices Web Portal (via CDN / Edge Cache → Web Application Firewall).
edges.exploitation_consequence: this service path is compromised, an attacker can pivot laterally to eServices Web Portal (via CDN / Edge Cache → Web Application Firewall).

edges.story_text: As a system administrator, I need administrative access to Admin Console from Government Staff to manage the system. If this control-plane path is abused, an attacker with lateral movement gains privileged control over Admin Console.
edges.exploitation_consequence: this control-plane path is abused, an attacker with lateral movement gains privileged control over Admin Console.

edges.story_text: Infrastructure routing: CDN / Edge Cache → Web Application Firewall. No user-facing story — transparent protection layer.

As a end user, I want to communicate between Web Application Firewall and eServices Web Portal to support the workflow. If this service path is compromised, an attacker can pivot laterally to eServices Web Portal.
edges.exploitation_consequence: this service path is compromised, an attacker can pivot laterally to eServices Web Portal.

edges.story_text: As a end user, I want to communicate between Web Application Firewall and API Gateway to support the workflow. If this service path is compromised, an attacker can pivot laterally to API Gateway.
edges.exploitation_consequence: this service path is compromised, an attacker can pivot laterally to API Gateway.

edges.story_text: As a service account, I want to communicate between eServices Web Portal and API Gateway to support the workflow. If this service path is compromised, an attacker can pivot laterally to API Gateway.
edges.exploitation_consequence: this service path is compromised, an attacker can pivot laterally to API Gateway.

edges.story_text: As a system administrator, I need administrative access to API Gateway from Admin Console to manage the system. If this control-plane path is abused, an attacker with lateral movement gains privileged control over API Gateway.
edges.exploitation_consequence: this control-plane path is abused, an attacker with lateral movement gains privileged control over API Gateway.

edges.story_text: As a service account, I want to authenticate via API Gateway to Auth Server OAuth2 so that I can access protected resources. If this flow is compromised, an attacker gains a valid identity and all access rights it carries.
edges.exploitation_consequence: this flow is compromised, an attacker gains a valid identity and all access rights it carries.

edges.story_text: As a service account, I want to authenticate via API Gateway to Lambda App - Application Processor so that I can access protected resources. If this flow is compromised, an attacker gains a valid identity and all access rights it carries.
edges.exploitation_consequence: this flow is compromised, an attacker gains a valid identity and all access rights it carries.

edges.story_text: As a service account, I want to communicate between API Gateway and Container App - Case Manager to support the workflow. If this service path is compromised, an attacker can pivot laterally to Container App - Case Manager.
edges.exploitation_consequence: this service path is compromised, an attacker can pivot laterally to Container App - Case Manager.

edges.story_text: As a user, I want to authenticate via Auth Server OAuth2 to Citizen Database so that I can access protected resources. If this flow is compromised, an attacker gains a valid identity and all access rights it carries.
edges.exploitation_consequence: this flow is compromised, an attacker gains a valid identity and all access rights it carries.

edges.story_text: As a service account, I want to authenticate via Lambda App - Application Processor to Citizen Database so that I can access protected resources. If this flow is compromised, an attacker gains a valid identity and all access rights it carries.
edges.exploitation_consequence: this flow is compromised, an attacker gains a valid identity and all access rights it carries.

edges.story_text: As a service account, I want to authenticate via Lambda App - Application Processor to Case Management DB so that I can access protected resources. If this flow is compromised, an attacker gains a valid identity and all access rights it carries.
edges.exploitation_consequence: this flow is compromised, an attacker gains a valid identity and all access rights it carries.

edges.story_text: As a service account, I want to authenticate via Lambda App - Application Processor to Lambda - Notification Service so that I can access protected resources. If this flow is compromised, an attacker gains a valid identity and all access rights it carries.
edges.exploitation_consequence: this flow is compromised, an attacker gains a valid identity and all access rights it carries.

edges.story_text: As a service account, I want to retrieve data from Citizen Database via Lambda - Notification Service so that I can fulfil my business need. If this path is exploited, an attacker can silently collect generic data.
edges.exploitation_consequence: this path is exploited, an attacker can silently collect generic data.

edges.story_text: As a service account, I need administrative access to Case Management DB from Lambda - Report Generator to manage the system. If this control-plane path is abused, an attacker with lateral movement gains privileged control over Case Management DB.
edges.exploitation_consequence: this control-plane path is abused, an attacker with lateral movement gains privileged control over Case Management DB.

edges.story_text: As a service account, I want to retrieve data from Document Store S3 via Lambda - Report Generator so that I can fulfil my business need. If this path is exploited, an attacker can silently collect generic data.
edges.exploitation_consequence: this path is exploited, an attacker can silently collect generic data.

edges.story_text: As a end user, I need administrative access to Case Management DB from Container App - Case Manager to manage the system. If this control-plane path is abused, an attacker with lateral movement gains privileged control over Case Management DB.
edges.exploitation_consequence: this control-plane path is abused, an attacker with lateral movement gains privileged control over Case Management DB.

edges.story_text: As a end user, I want to communicate between Container App - Case Manager and App Server - Workflow Engine to support the workflow. If this service path is compromised, an attacker can pivot laterally to App Server - Workflow Engine.
edges.exploitation_consequence: this service path is compromised, an attacker can pivot laterally to App Server - Workflow Engine.

edges.story_text: As a end user, I want to communicate between App Server - Workflow Engine and Lambda - Report Generator to support the workflow. If this service path is compromised, an attacker can pivot laterally to Lambda - Report Generator.
edges.exploitation_consequence: this service path is compromised, an attacker can pivot laterally to Lambda - Report Generator.

edges.story_text: As a end user, I want to retrieve data from Audit Log DynamoDB via App Server - Workflow Engine so that I can fulfil my business need. If this path is exploited, an attacker can silently collect generic data.
edges.exploitation_consequence: this path is exploited, an attacker can silently collect generic data.

journeys.user_goal: authenticate and access Case Management DB
journeys.business_value: Enables system administrator to authenticate and access Case Management DB — core to the system's intended function.
journeys.story_text: Government Staff connect through Admin Console and API Gateway and Lambda App - Application Processor to Case Management DB. This is a normal, intended workflow. An attacker who gains a foothold at Government Staff can follow the same path through Admin Console and API Gateway and Lambda App - Application Processor and reach Case Management DB — blending with legitimate traffic.
journeys.exploitation_chain: Gain initial access at Government Staff. Access Admin Console via the initial entry point. Move from Admin Console to API Gateway. Move from API Gateway to Lambda App - Application Processor. Reach target Case Management DB — exfiltrate, encrypt, or disrupt.

journeys.business_value: Enables system administrator to authenticate and access Citizen Database — core to the system's intended function.
journeys.story_text: Government Staff connect through Admin Console and API Gateway and Auth Server OAuth2 to Citizen Database. This is a normal, intended workflow. An attacker who gains a foothold at Government Staff can follow the same path through Admin Console and API Gateway and Auth Server OAuth2 and reach Citizen Database — blending with legitimate traffic.
journeys.exploitation_chain: Gain initial access at Government Staff. Access Admin Console via the initial entry point. Move from Admin Console to API Gateway. Move from API Gateway to Auth Server OAuth2. Reach target Citizen Database — exfiltrate, encrypt, or disrupt.

journeys.user_goal: read data from CDN / Edge Cache
journeys.business_value: Enables end user to read data from CDN / Edge Cache — core to the system's intended function.
journeys.story_text: An end user connects via Citizens / Browser to CDN / Edge Cache. This is a normal, intended workflow. An attacker who gains a foothold at Citizens / Browser can follow the same path and reach CDN / Edge Cache — blending with legitimate traffic.
journeys.exploitation_chain: Gain initial access at Citizens / Browser. Reach target CDN / Edge Cache — exfiltrate, encrypt, or disrupt.

journeys.user_goal: read data from CDN / Edge Cache
journeys.business_value: Enables end user to read data from CDN / Edge Cache — core to the system's intended function.
journeys.story_text: An end user connects via Mobile App iOS/Android to CDN / Edge Cache. This is a normal, intended workflow. An attacker who gains a foothold at Mobile App iOS/Android can follow the same path and reach CDN / Edge Cache — blending with legitimate traffic.
journeys.exploitation_chain: Gain initial access at Mobile App iOS/Android. Reach target CDN / Edge Cache — exfiltrate, encrypt, or disrupt.

journeys.business_value: Enables system administrator to administer Audit Log DynamoDB — core to the system's intended function.
journeys.story_text: Government Staff connect through Admin Console and API Gateway and Container App - Case Manager and App Server - Workflow Engine to Audit Log DynamoDB. This is a normal, intended workflow. An attacker who gains a foothold at Government Staff can follow the same path through Admin Console and API Gateway and Container App - Case Manager and App Server - Workflow Engine and reach Audit Log DynamoDB — blending with legitimate traffic.
journeys.exploitation_chain: Gain initial access at Government Staff. Access Admin Console via the initial entry point. Move from Admin Console to API Gateway. Move from API Gateway to Container App - Case Manager. Move from Container App - Case Manager to App Server - Workflow Engine. Reach target Audit Log DynamoDB — exfiltrate, encrypt, or disrupt.

journeys.user_goal: read data from Document Store S3
journeys.business_value: Enables end user to read data from Document Store S3 — core to the system's intended function.
journeys.story_text: An end user connects via Citizens / Browser through CDN / Edge Cache and Web Application Firewall and API Gateway and Container App - Case Manager and App Server - Workflow Engine and Lambda - Report Generator to Document Store S3. This is a normal, intended workflow. An attacker who gains a foothold at Citizens / Browser can follow the same path through CDN / Edge Cache and Web Application Firewall and API Gateway and Container App - Case Manager and App Server - Workflow Engine and Lambda - Report Generator and reach Document Store S3 — blending with legitimate traffic.
journeys.exploitation_chain: Gain initial access at Citizens / Browser. Access CDN / Edge Cache via the initial entry point. Move from CDN / Edge Cache to Web Application Firewall. Move from Web Application Firewall to API Gateway. Move from API Gateway to Container App - Case Manager. Move from Container App - Case Manager to App Server - Workflow Engine. Move from App Server - Workflow Engine to Lambda - Report Generator. Reach target Document Store S3 — exfiltrate, encrypt, or disrupt.

Insider Threat (risk=60/100): Limit insider damage potential

primary.title: Principle of Least Privilege
primary.statement: Deny access by default and grant only the minimum permissions required for authorised accounts or processes to perform a specific function based on the account inventory implemented.
primary.recommendation: Consider attribute- or feature-based access control for greater customisability and granularity. Use automated tools such as AWS IAM Access Advisor or Azure AD Access Review to assist with granular permission management.
primary.risk_statement: Violating the principle of least privileges increases the risk of unauthorised access, privilege escalation, and potential security breaches due to unnecessary permissions, compromising the overall security posture.

secondaries.title: Inactive and Expired Accounts
secondaries.statement: Disable or remove [ insert: param, ac-3_prm_3 ] accounts within [ insert: param, ac-3_prm_1 ] day(s) from last day of authorised use or have not been used for [ insert: param, ac-3_prm_2 ] day(s).
secondaries.risk_statement: Failure to disable or remove unused accounts or credentials with elevated access increases the risk of unauthorised access, as dormant accounts may become targets for exploitation, compromising the security of the system.
secondaries.recommendation: Use automated checks to identify accounts and credentials that should be disabled. Consider using automated workflows such as System for Cross-domain Identity Management (SCIM) or identity lifecycle management tools. For cloud service provider accounts, use tools such as AWS Config iam-user-unused-credentials-check to manage Identity and Access Management (IAM) Users.

control_recommendations.rationale: RAPIDS: Phishing, Insider Threat | Confirmed by attack path(s) #1, #2, #3, #4, #5
detailed_rationale: Phishing (risk=65/100): Detect anomalous authentication patterns
Insider Threat (risk=60/100): Audit trail for forensics and detection
control_recommendations.enhanced_rationale: Mitigates T1021 in path(s) #1, #2, #5, T1027 in path(s) #1, #2, #5

primary.title: Separate Log Storage
primary.statement: Store logs in a different system or system component than the system component that generated the logs.
primary.recommendation: Do not store logs only in the same system component that generated it. For example, an application server on EC2 or ECS should send logs to a separate storage such as an S3 bucket as soon as possible after the logged event instead of only storing it on the server. For cloud audit logs, store them in a separate system or account.
primary.risk_statement: Storing logs in a repository separate from the system component reduces the risk of tampering, unauthorised access, and manipulation of logs if the system component is compromised.

secondaries.title: Network Flow Logging
secondaries.statement: Log network traffic going to and from network interfaces.
secondaries.risk_statement: Failing to log network traffic going to and from network interfaces increases the risk of overlooking suspicious activities, potential security breaches, and the inability to trace and investigate network-related incidents effectively.
secondaries.recommendation: Enable VPC Flow Logs for AWS or its equivalents.

control_recommendations.rationale: RAPIDS: Supply Chain, Application Vulns | Confirmed by attack path(s) #1, #2, #3, #4, #5
detailed_rationale: Supply Chain (risk=70/100): Continuous dependency monitoring
Application Vulns (risk=50/100): Continuous security assessment
control_recommendations.enhanced_rationale: Mitigates T1021 in path(s) #1, #2, #5, T1027 in path(s) #1, #2, #5

primary.title: Vulnerability Assessment
primary.statement: Run regular [ insert: param, st-1_prm_1 ] vulnerability assessment scans for eligible hosts at least every [ insert: param, st-1_prm_2 ] day(s).
primary.recommendation: Select agent-based or network-based scans as necessary. Implement authenticated scans where possible for greater coverage. Use scanners such as Amazon Inspector or Microsoft Defender for Cloud for continuous scanning of cloud systems. For on-premises systems or systems that require periodic scans, subscribe to Vulnerability Management System (VMS). For SaaS, refer to past vulnerability scanning reports done by the SaaS provider, if any.
primary.risk_statement: Without regular vulnerability assessment scans, hosts remain exposed to undetected security vulnerabilities or misconfigurations, increasing the risk of exploitation and unauthorised access to critical systems.

secondaries.title: Automated Patch Management Tools
secondaries.statement: Use automated patch management tools to facilitate security patching of operating systems and software included in the operating systems.
secondaries.risk_statement: Failure to automate security patching of operating systems and included software increases the risk of delayed or missed security updates, leaving systems vulnerable to known exploits and potential security breaches, compromising the overall security of the environment.
secondaries.recommendation: Patch management can be automated as part of the build and deploy phase, such as with golden images for containers and virtual machines. It can also be automated in running instances on the cloud via CSP node management services (such as Azure Update Manager or AWS Systems Manager Patch Manager) and on-premises via patch management services (such as Windows Server Update Services or Configuration Management tools). To minimise risk of disruption due to patching, consider staged rollouts with canary deployments or in non-production environments.

control_recommendations.rationale: RAPIDS: Dos, Application Vulns | Confirmed by attack path(s) #1, #2, #3, #4, #5
detailed_rationale: Dos (risk=60/100): Prevent resource exhaustion
Application Vulns (risk=50/100): Prevent brute force and enumeration

primary.title: Secure Inter-Service Communication
primary.statement: Ensure communications between services are secure by making them authenticated, authorised and encrypted.
primary.recommendation: Design and build inter-service communications (e.g., databases, microservices) to be authenticated, authorised and encrypted (e.g., via API gateways, proxies, private endpoint services, message queues, or service meshes). It is recommended to log communication (such as access logs, transaction logs or payloads) between services for detection, monitoring and investigation of incidents.
primary.risk_statement: Failure to ensure secure communications between services increases the risk of unauthorised access, data breaches, and potential manipulation of sensitive information during transit.

secondaries.title: Output Sanitisation
secondaries.statement: Sanitise all application outputs that will be used to render a HTML document.
secondaries.risk_statement: Lack of sanitisation for application outputs used in rendering HTML documents exposes the system to the risk of cross-site scripting (XSS) attacks, allowing malicious code execution in browsers.
secondaries.recommendation: Any application outputs that are returned to the requester and used to render a HTML document can lead to cross-site scripting (XSS) attacks if they contain special characters that change the rendering of the HTML document by the browser.

control_recommendations.rationale: RAPIDS: Ransomware | Confirmed by attack path(s) #1, #2, #3, #4, #5
detailed_rationale: Ransomware (risk=85/100): Data recovery is MANDATORY for ransomware resilience
control_recommendations.enhanced_rationale: Mitigates T1485 in path(s) #1, #2, #3, T1486 in path(s) #1, #2, #3

primary.title: Backup
primary.statement: Backup all important data and systems at least every [ insert: param, br-1_prm_1 ] day(s), and store backups in a secure and separate location.
primary.recommendation: Use default CSP-managed backup services (e.g., AWS Backup, Azure Backup, GCP Backup and DR Service). Consider alternative backup services only when default CSP services cannot be used. Store backups and snapshots separately to primary data storage with data encrypted-at-rest. For SaaS, ensure that SaaS provider is able to restore last backup.
primary.risk_statement: Without regular backups stored in a secure and separate location, there is an increased risk of data loss, system failures, and extended downtime in the event of accidental deletion, hardware failures, or malicious attacks.

secondaries.title: Backup Retention
secondaries.statement: Prevent backups from being modified or deleted for [ insert: param, br-3_prm_1 ] day(s) or as stipulated in the agency's data retention policies.
secondaries.risk_statement: Lack of prevention measures against the modification or deletion of backups for the specified duration increases the risk of data loss, unauthorised alterations, and potential inability to recover from incidents, compromising the integrity and availability of critical information.
secondaries.recommendation: Use S3 Object Lock or Azure Blob Storage with immutability enabled to enforce time-based retention policies.

control_recommendations.rationale: RAPIDS: Ransomware | Confirmed by attack path(s) #1, #2, #3, #4, #5
detailed_rationale: Ransomware (risk=85/100): Detect ransomware behavior before encryption
Depth: PREVENTION at device layer (At App Server - Workflow Engine hop)
Depth: MITIGATION:DETECT at device layer (At App Server - Workflow Engine hop)

primary.title: Host Security Event Logging
primary.statement: Log security events on hosts.
primary.recommendation: Host security events include operating system security events, authentication, and endpoint detection and response alerts, configuration changes, and account and access rights changes.
primary.risk_statement: Neglecting to log security events on hosts increases the risk of undetected security incidents, compromises incident response capabilities, and hinders forensic analysis, limiting the ability to identify and mitigate potential threats.

secondaries.title: Network Flow Logging
secondaries.statement: Log network traffic going to and from network interfaces.
secondaries.risk_statement: Failing to log network traffic going to and from network interfaces increases the risk of overlooking suspicious activities, potential security breaches, and the inability to trace and investigate network-related incidents effectively.
secondaries.recommendation: Enable VPC Flow Logs for AWS or its equivalents.

primary.title: Peer Review
primary.statement: Require peer review and approval by a designated reviewer before merging into the default branch.
primary.recommendation: Use GitLab's protected branch and merge request settings, GitHub's branch protection settings or similar code repository controls to enforce this.
primary.risk_statement: Without peer review and approval before merging, there is an increased risk of introducing undetected coding errors, security vulnerabilities, and maintaining codebase consistency may become challenging.

primary.title: Singpass/Corppass for Public Users
primary.statement: Use Singpass or Corppass MFA for digital services that require high level of identity assurance for Public Users.
primary.recommendation: For high impact or high risk transactions, use Singpass/Corppass to identify Public Users (e.g. citizens). Agency or internal Users should use Government managed Single Sign-on (SSO) solutions (such as WOG AAD).
primary.risk_statement: Leverage on Singpass or Corppass to reduce duplication of effort and provide consistent end user experience.

secondaries.title: Static Credential Expiry and Rotation
secondaries.statement: Rotate long-lived static credentials such as API keys, access keys, and personal access tokens every [ insert: param, ac-13_prm_1 ] day(s) or use time-restricted credentials.
secondaries.risk_statement: Failure to regularly rotate long-lived credentials or use time-restricted credentials increases the risk of unauthorised access from stolen or unrevoked credentials.
secondaries.recommendation: Automate credential rotation where possible. Consider time-restricted alternatives to long-lived static credentials, such as AWS Security Token Service and IAM Identity Center authentication instead of IAM user access keys.

control_recommendations.rationale: RAPIDS: Insider Threat | Confirmed by attack path(s) #1, #2, #3, #4, #5
detailed_rationale: Insider Threat (risk=60/100): Detect anomalous user behavior
control_recommendations.enhanced_rationale: Mitigates T1027 in path(s) #1, #2, #5, T1059 in path(s) #1, #2, #5

primary.title: User and Entity Behaviour Analytics
primary.statement: Implement User and Entity Behaviour Analytics (UEBA) to monitor and analyse user activities for suspicious behaviour and potential threats.
primary.recommendation: Select a UEBA tool that integrates with existing security information and event management (SIEM) solutions and provides real-time alerts for anomalous activities. Ensure regular updates and tuning of the tool to enhance detection capabilities.
primary.risk_statement: Lack of monitoring for potentially malicious user and entity behaviour increases the risk of insider threats and undetected malicious activities, potentially leading to data breaches and system compromise.

secondaries.title: Host Security Event Logging
secondaries.risk_statement: Neglecting to log security events on hosts increases the risk of undetected security incidents, compromises incident response capabilities, and hinders forensic analysis, limiting the ability to identify and mitigate potential threats.
secondaries.recommendation: Host security events include operating system security events, authentication, and endpoint detection and response alerts, configuration changes, and account and access rights changes.

control_recommendations.rationale: RAPIDS: Application Vulns | Confirmed by attack path(s) #1, #2, #3, #4, #5
detailed_rationale: Application Vulns (risk=50/100): Fix known CVEs in application stack
control_recommendations.enhanced_rationale: Mitigates T1021 in path(s) #1, #2, #5, T1027 in path(s) #1, #2, #5

primary.title: Automated Patch Management Tools
primary.statement: Use automated patch management tools to facilitate security patching of operating systems and software included in the operating systems.
primary.recommendation: Patch management can be automated as part of the build and deploy phase, such as with golden images for containers and virtual machines. It can also be automated in running instances on the cloud via CSP node management services (such as Azure Update Manager or AWS Systems Manager Patch Manager) and on-premises via patch management services (such as Windows Server Update Services or Configuration Management tools). To minimise risk of disruption due to patching, consider staged rollouts with canary deployments or in non-production environments.
primary.risk_statement: Failure to automate security patching of operating systems and included software increases the risk of delayed or missed security updates, leaving systems vulnerable to known exploits and potential security breaches, compromising the overall security of the environment.

secondaries.title: Host System Hardening
secondaries.statement: Harden the host configuration with reference to industry standards.
secondaries.risk_statement: Without hardening the operating system configuration according to industry standards, there's an increased risk of security vulnerabilities, unauthorised access, and potential exploitation, compromising the overall security posture and resilience of the operating system.
secondaries.recommendation: Select the appropriate benchmark for the host such as from the NIST National Checklist Program or CIS Benchmarks . Automate the configuration process or use hardened images instead of manually configuring.

control_recommendations.rationale: RAPIDS: Application Vulns | Confirmed by attack path(s) #1, #2, #5, #6
detailed_rationale: Application Vulns (risk=50/100): Prevent injection attacks
control_recommendations.enhanced_rationale: Mitigates T1068 in path(s) #1, #2, #5, T1190 in path(s) #1, #2, #5

primary.title: Output Sanitisation
primary.statement: Sanitise all application outputs that will be used to render a HTML document.
primary.recommendation: Any application outputs that are returned to the requester and used to render a HTML document can lead to cross-site scripting (XSS) attacks if they contain special characters that change the rendering of the HTML document by the browser.
primary.risk_statement: Lack of sanitisation for application outputs used in rendering HTML documents exposes the system to the risk of cross-site scripting (XSS) attacks, allowing malicious code execution in browsers.

primary.title: Security Awareness Training
primary.statement: Conduct security awareness training for all employees that operate and manage the system every [ insert: param, hr-1_prm_1 ] months.
primary.recommendation: Develop a comprehensive security awareness training that covers: (a) Relevant laws, regulations, codes of practice, policies, standards, guidelines and procedures (b) Phishing and social engineering awareness (c) Incident reporting procedures (d) Secure handling of sensitive information Utilise other methods such as posters, emails to provide regular and timely communication covering cybersecurity awareness messages and prevailing threats, impacts and mitigations.
primary.risk_statement: Inadequate or infrequent security awareness programs increase the likelihood of successful social engineering attacks or data breaches and non-compliance due to human error or negligence.

primary.title: Vulnerability Assessment
primary.statement: Run regular [ insert: param, st-1_prm_1 ] vulnerability assessment scans for eligible hosts at least every [ insert: param, st-1_prm_2 ] day(s).
primary.recommendation: Select agent-based or network-based scans as necessary. Implement authenticated scans where possible for greater coverage. Use scanners such as Amazon Inspector or Microsoft Defender for Cloud for continuous scanning of cloud systems. For on-premises systems or systems that require periodic scans, subscribe to Vulnerability Management System (VMS). For SaaS, refer to past vulnerability scanning reports done by the SaaS provider, if any.
primary.risk_statement: Without regular vulnerability assessment scans, hosts remain exposed to undetected security vulnerabilities or misconfigurations, increasing the risk of exploitation and unauthorised access to critical systems.

primary.title: Secret Detection
primary.statement: Set up secret detection in the [ insert: param, sd-6_prm_1 ] and remediate true positives within [ insert: param, sd-6_prm_2 ] day(s).
primary.recommendation: Ensure that the exposed secret is revoked and purged from the Git history.
primary.risk_statement: Without setting up secret detection and addressing true positive findings promptly, there's an increased risk of exposing sensitive information, potential unauthorised access, and compromised security.

secondaries.title: CI Environment Variable Secrets Management
secondaries.statement: Protect environment variable secrets used in CI jobs by limiting them to protected pipelines and masking them in job logs.
secondaries.risk_statement: Failing to protect environment variable secrets in CI jobs by limiting them to protected pipelines and masking them in job logs increases the risk of unauthorised access and exposure of sensitive information.
secondaries.recommendation: Use GitLab's CI/CD variable security settings or GitHub's encrypted secrets with the add-mask workflow command.

secondaries.title: Secrets Management
secondaries.statement: Securely store secrets in an appropriate secrets management solution with access control enforcement, encryption, and monitoring.
secondaries.risk_statement: Exposure of sensitive information and unauthorised access to system credentials may occur if application secrets are stored insecurely or hard-coded in source code.
secondaries.recommendation: Secrets include API keys, access keys, and other static credentials. Do not store secrets unencrypted in source code or configuration files. Store secrets in cloud-native solutions like AWS Secrets Manager and Azure Key Vault or cloud-agnostic solutions like HashiCorp Vault and CyberArk Conjur. For SaaS or platforms, ensure that secrets are stored in an appropriate solution. For example, use GitHub Actions secrets instead of variables.

secondaries.title: Runtime Container Secrets
secondaries.statement: Provide secrets and sensitive data to the container at runtime instead of image build time.
secondaries.risk_statement: Providing secrets and sensitive data to the container at runtime instead of image build time reduces the risk of exposing sensitive information in the image and enhances security by ensuring that secrets are managed and updated independently, minimising the risk of unauthorised access or data compromise.
secondaries.recommendation: Ensure no secrets (e.g., TLS certificate keys, cloud provider credentials, SSH private keys, database passwords) are embedded in the container image by using dedicated features like Docker secrets or podman-secret-create.

control_recommendations.coverage_note: No MITRE ATT&CK techniques (preventive control for _metadata)
detailed_rationale: Phishing (risk=65/100): Filter phishing emails before delivery

primary.title: Secure Inter-Service Communication
primary.statement: Ensure communications between services are secure by making them authenticated, authorised and encrypted.
primary.recommendation: Design and build inter-service communications (e.g., databases, microservices) to be authenticated, authorised and encrypted (e.g., via API gateways, proxies, private endpoint services, message queues, or service meshes). It is recommended to log communication (such as access logs, transaction logs or payloads) between services for detection, monitoring and investigation of incidents.
primary.risk_statement: Failure to ensure secure communications between services increases the risk of unauthorised access, data breaches, and potential manipulation of sensitive information during transit.

secondaries.title: Network and System Component Segmentation
secondaries.statement: Segment and isolate system components into separate physical and logical networks or environments based on their security requirements and risk levels.
secondaries.risk_statement: Failure to segregate private and public resources within distinct subnets in a virtual network increases the risk of unauthorised access to sensitive data, as private resources may be exposed to the public internet, compromising the overall security of the infrastructure.
secondaries.recommendation: Deploy security controls such as firewalls, security groups, and API gateways to establish and maintain segmentation. In cloud environments, implement a tiered network architecture where internet-facing services are restricted to designated public segments, while sensitive backend systems remain in private segments with controlled outbound access. Consider micro-segmentation for granular control based on workload sensitivity.

control_recommendations.name: dlp
control_recommendations.rationale: Addresses 3 technique(s) via 1 mitigation(s): T1005, T1041, T1567

primary.title: Data Residency
primary.statement: Enforce data residency of [ insert: param, dp-1_prm_2 ] data in [ insert: param, dp-1_prm_1 ].
primary.recommendation: Data residency in Singapore must be enforced for [ insert: param, dp-1_prm_2 ] data.
primary.risk_statement: Failure to enforce data residency in the appropriate country may lead to legal and regulatory compliance issues, privacy concerns, and potential unauthorised access or storage of sensitive data outside the jurisdiction, increasing the risk of legal consequences and data breaches.

secondaries.title: Separate Log Storage
secondaries.statement: Store logs in a different system or system component than the system component that generated the logs.
secondaries.risk_statement: Storing logs in a repository separate from the system component reduces the risk of tampering, unauthorised access, and manipulation of logs if the system component is compromised.
secondaries.recommendation: Do not store logs only in the same system component that generated it. For example, an application server on EC2 or ECS should send logs to a separate storage such as an S3 bucket as soon as possible after the logged event instead of only storing it on the server. For cloud audit logs, store them in a separate system or account.

primary.title: Secure Inter-Service Communication
primary.statement: Ensure communications between services are secure by making them authenticated, authorised and encrypted.
primary.recommendation: Design and build inter-service communications (e.g., databases, microservices) to be authenticated, authorised and encrypted (e.g., via API gateways, proxies, private endpoint services, message queues, or service meshes). It is recommended to log communication (such as access logs, transaction logs or payloads) between services for detection, monitoring and investigation of incidents.
primary.risk_statement: Failure to ensure secure communications between services increases the risk of unauthorised access, data breaches, and potential manipulation of sensitive information during transit.

secondaries.title: Network and System Component Segmentation
secondaries.statement: Segment and isolate system components into separate physical and logical networks or environments based on their security requirements and risk levels.
secondaries.risk_statement: Failure to segregate private and public resources within distinct subnets in a virtual network increases the risk of unauthorised access to sensitive data, as private resources may be exposed to the public internet, compromising the overall security of the infrastructure.
secondaries.recommendation: Deploy security controls such as firewalls, security groups, and API gateways to establish and maintain segmentation. In cloud environments, implement a tiered network architecture where internet-facing services are restricted to designated public segments, while sensitive backend systems remain in private segments with controlled outbound access. Consider micro-segmentation for granular control based on workload sensitivity.

primary.title: Secure Inter-Service Communication
primary.statement: Ensure communications between services are secure by making them authenticated, authorised and encrypted.
primary.recommendation: Design and build inter-service communications (e.g., databases, microservices) to be authenticated, authorised and encrypted (e.g., via API gateways, proxies, private endpoint services, message queues, or service meshes). It is recommended to log communication (such as access logs, transaction logs or payloads) between services for detection, monitoring and investigation of incidents.
primary.risk_statement: Failure to ensure secure communications between services increases the risk of unauthorised access, data breaches, and potential manipulation of sensitive information during transit.

secondaries.title: Inter-Private Network Connectivity
secondaries.statement: Route network traffic between private networks without going through the internet.
secondaries.risk_statement: When routing through the internet, there's an increased risk of man-in-the-middle and spoofing attacks. Allowing bidirectional access between networks without fine-grained access controls increases the risk of unauthorised access, potential data exfiltration, and compromise of network security compared to unidirectional access to specific resources.
secondaries.recommendation: Use CSP Private endpoint services (e.g., AWS PrivateLink with VPC endpoints) when you want to allow one or more consumer VPCs unidirectional access to a specific service or set of instances in the service provider VPC. Otherwise, use VPC peering and Transit Gateway when you want to enable layer-3 IP connectivity between VPCs. Refer to the Multi-VPC AWS Network Infrastructure Whitepaper for further guidance.

control_recommendations.name: NETWORK MONITORING
control_recommendations.description: Remote System Discovery — detect internal reconnaissance via network scan alerts

primary.title: Network Flow Logging
primary.statement: Log network traffic going to and from network interfaces.
primary.recommendation: Enable VPC Flow Logs for AWS or its equivalents.
primary.risk_statement: Failing to log network traffic going to and from network interfaces increases the risk of overlooking suspicious activities, potential security breaches, and the inability to trace and investigate network-related incidents effectively.

control_recommendations.name: FILE INTEGRITY MONITORING
control_recommendations.description: File and Directory Discovery — monitor for enumeration of sensitive paths

primary.title: Network Flow Logging
primary.statement: Log network traffic going to and from network interfaces.
primary.recommendation: Enable VPC Flow Logs for AWS or its equivalents.
primary.risk_statement: Failing to log network traffic going to and from network interfaces increases the risk of overlooking suspicious activities, potential security breaches, and the inability to trace and investigate network-related incidents effectively.

expected_attack_paths.rationale: [CRITICAL] Government Staff → Admin Console → API Gateway → Lambda App - Application Processor → Case Management DB. Legitimate use: system administrator traverse path to reach Case Management DB. If exploited: Gain initial access at Government Staff. Access Admin Console via the initial entry point. Move from Admin Console to API Gateway. Move from API Gateway to Lambda App - Application Processor. Reach target Case Management DB — exfiltrate, encrypt, or disrupt. Corroborated: user journey matches attack path.

[CRITICAL] Government Staff → Admin Console → API Gateway → Auth Server OAuth2 → Citizen Database. Legitimate use: system administrator traverse path to reach Citizen Database. If exploited: Gain initial access at Government Staff. Access Admin Console via the initial entry point. Move from Admin Console to API Gateway. Move from API Gateway to Auth Server OAuth2. Reach target Citizen Database — exfiltrate, encrypt, or disrupt. Corroborated: user journey matches attack path.

[CRITICAL] Citizens / Browser → CDN / Edge Cache. Legitimate use: end user traverse path to reach CDN / Edge Cache. If exploited: Gain initial access at Citizens / Browser. Reach target CDN / Edge Cache — exfiltrate, encrypt, or disrupt. Corroborated: user journey matches attack path.

[CRITICAL] Mobile App iOS/Android → CDN / Edge Cache. Legitimate use: end user traverse path to reach CDN / Edge Cache. If exploited: Gain initial access at Mobile App iOS/Android. Reach target CDN / Edge Cache — exfiltrate, encrypt, or disrupt. Corroborated: user journey matches attack path.

[HIGH] Government Staff → Admin Console → API Gateway → Container App - Case Manager → App Server - Workflow Engine → Audit Log DynamoDB. Legitimate use: system administrator traverse path to reach Audit Log DynamoDB. If exploited: Gain initial access at Government Staff. Access Admin Console via the initial entry point. Move from Admin Console to API Gateway. Move from API Gateway to Container App - Case Manager. Move from Container App - Case Manager to App Server - Workflow Engine. Reach target Audit Log DynamoDB — exfiltrate, encrypt, or disrupt. Corroborated: user journey matches attack path.

[MEDIUM] Citizens / Browser → CDN / Edge Cache → Web Application Firewall → API Gateway → Container App - Case Manager → App Server - Workflow Engine → Lambda - Report Generator → Document Store S3. Legitimate use: end user traverse path to reach Document Store S3. If exploited: Gain initial access at Citizens / Browser. Access CDN / Edge Cache via the initial entry point. Move from CDN / Edge Cache to Web Application Firewall. Move from Web Application Firewall to API Gateway. Move from API Gateway to Container App - Case Manager. Move from Container App - Case Manager to App Server - Workflow Engine. Move from App Server - Workflow Engine to Lambda - Report Generator. Reach target Document Store S3 — exfiltrate, encrypt, or disrupt. Corroborated: user journey matches attack path.

rationale: Minimal security controls - significant gaps. 6 attack paths identified. Control coverage: 29%.
